revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23985667504|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[ja:鉢塚古墳 (池田市)]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = (Presumed) {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Hachizuka Kofun | image = [[File:Hachizuka Kofun (Ikeda), genshitsu-1-2.jpg|280px]]
{{ill|Stone Chamber|ja|石室|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone Chamber|qq=}} {{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshitsu|qq=}}
(13-storied pagoda in the center (National {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qid=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}})) | alternate_name = | part_of = | location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|qid=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|Ikeda, Osaka|en|Ikeda, Osaka|qid=Q817965|lt=Ikeda, Osaka|1=Ikeda, Osaka}}鉢塚2丁目
（{{ill|Gosha Shrine (Ikeda)|ja|五社神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosha Shrine (Ikeda)|qq=}}境内） | coordinates deg = 34| coordinates min = 49| coordinates sec = 3.70 | coordinates deg = 135| coordinates min = 26| coordinates sec = 13.50 | omitted = JP-27 | area = Diameter 45m | burial chamber = {{ill|Corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} with lateral sleeves
(possibly housing a composite-style Sarcophagus) | excavations = | Accompanying tombs = | built = Late {{ill|6th century|en|6th century|qid=Q8090|lt=6th century|1=6th century}} to early {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|qid=Q8089|lt=7th century|1=7th century}} | Interred_individual = | Imperial tomb = | designation1 = Osaka Prefectural Historic Site "Hachizuka Kofun" | Tangible designation1 = | notes = | map = Japan Osaka | type = 円墳 | map_dot_label = Hachizuka Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Hachizuka Kofun''' (鉢塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun ({{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|qid=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}}) located in the {{ill|Hachizuka district|ja|鉢塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachizuka district|1=Hachizuka district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q86738459}} of {{ill|Ikeda, Osaka|en|Ikeda, Osaka|qid=Q817965|lt=Ikeda, Osaka|1=Ikeda, Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|qid=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. The tumulus is presumed to be a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and is Historic Site1 as a historic site by Osaka Prefecture. == Overview == [[File:Gosha-jinja (Ikeda), entrance.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Gosha Shrine (Ikeda)|ja|五社神社_(池田市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosha Shrine (Ikeda)|qq=}}}}]] The Hachizuka Kofun is a large Kofun located on the edge of a hill derived from the {{ill|Satsukiyama|ja|五月山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satsukiyama|1=Mount Satsuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11372460}} range on the eastern bank of the {{ill|Inagawa River|ja|猪名川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inagawa River|1=Ina River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11571867}} in northern Osaka Prefecture{{Sfn|Ikeda City Archaeological Report 2001|2002}}. It is currently situated within the precincts of {{ill|Gosha Shrine (Ikeda)|ja|五社神社_(池田市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosha Shrine (Ikeda)|qq=}}. The name "Hachizuka" is believed to derive either from the resemblance of its mound to an overturned bowl or from a bowl discovered inside the stone chamber{{Sfn|Ikeda City History Vol. 1|1997|pp=193-198,214-215}}. An excavation survey was conducted in 2001. The tumulus is estimated to be circular (previously identified as a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|上円下方墳|1=Joenkahofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Joenkahofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) with a diameter of approximately 45 meters{{Sfn|Ikeda City Archaeological Report 2001|2002}}{{Sfn|Kofun built of the Inagawa Basin|2010}}. The mound was built in two tiers, and a later Kofun with relics (Historic Site1 as an Important designation1 by Ikeda City) was constructed atop it{{Sfn|Kofun built of the Inagawa Basin|2010}}. Surrounding the mound is a ditch approximately 7 meters wide and 0.8 meters deep, bringing the total diameter, including the ditch, to about 62 meters{{Sfn|Ikeda City Archaeological Report 2001|2002}}. The burial chamber is a {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} with lateral sleeves, oriented southward. It is constructed with large megaliths, measuring 14.88 meters in total length and 5.2 meters in height, Ranking among the largest of its kind in Japan{{Sfn|Kofun built of the Inagawa Basin|2010}}. The chamber is thought to have housed a composite-style Sarcophagus{{Sfn|Ikeda City History Vol. 1|1997|pp=193-198,214-215}}, and later, a thirteen-tiered pagoda (Historic Site1 as a National Important designation1), slab monuments, and stone statues were enshrined within{{Sfn|Kofun built of the Inagawa Basin|2010}}. Due to early looting, the grave goods are not well-documented{{Sfn|Hachizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The construction period is estimated to be from the late [[Kofun period|Kofun era]] to the early {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|qid=Q210688|lt=Asuka era|1=Asuka period}} (late 6th to early 7th century){{Sfn|Kofun built of the Inagawa Basin|2010}}. Hachizuka Kofun is considered a chieftain's tomb, built after a generational gap following the nearby {{ill|Futagozuka Kofun (Ikeda)|lt=Futagozuka Kofun (Ikeda)|lt=Futagotsuka Kofun (Ikeda)|ja|二子塚古墳_(池田市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futagotsuka Kofun (Ikeda)|qq=}} (6th century), with connections to the chieftains' tombs on the western Inagawa Riverbank, such as {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (disambiguation)|lt=Shiratori Kofun (disambiguation)|lt=Shiratori Kofun (Takarazuka)|ja|白鳥塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun (Takarazuka)|qq=}} (Mi11le Mountain Kofun, {{ill|Takarazuka, Hyōgo|en|Takarazuka, Hyōgo|qid=Q823967|lt=Takarazuka|1=Takarazuka, Hyōgo}}). Both demonstrate similarities to stone chambers found in the Yamato region, indicating cultural exchanges{{Sfn|Kofun built of the Inagawa Basin|2010}}. While the identity of the interred is unknown, the Ikeda area, historically known as Qin Township (秦上郷・秦下郷), is closely associated with the {{ill|Hata clan|en|Hata clan|qid=Q987290|lt=Hata clan|1=Hata clan}}, leading to the theory that the tomb belonged to a Hata chieftain{{Sfn|Ikeda City History Vol. 1|1997|pp=193-198,214-215}}{{Sfn|Kofun built of the Inagawa Basin|2010}}. The site was Historic Site1 as a historic site by Osaka Prefecture in 1970. == Historic Site == === National Important designation1 === * Thirteen-Tier Pagoda of Gosha Shrine (Building) *: Constructed in the early {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|qid=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period|1=Muromachi period}}. Owned by Gosha Shrine. It was recognized as an Important designation1 in 1959{{National designation1 Database|102|2155|Thirteen-Tier Pagoda of Gosha Shrine}}[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html List of Historic Site in Osaka Prefecture] (Osaka Prefecture website, updated November 21, 2018).. === Osaka Prefectural Historic Site === * Hachizuka Kofun - Designated on December 7, 1970. === Ikeda City {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qid=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}} === * burial goods from the Kofun (Archaeological Material) *: These include relics from the Kamakura period Kofun atop Hachizuka Kofun. == See Also == * {{ill|Futagozuka Kofun (Ikeda)|lt=Futagozuka Kofun (Ikeda)|lt=Futagotsuka Kofun (Ikeda)|ja|二子塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futagotsuka Kofun (Ikeda)|qq=}} * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (disambiguation)|lt=Shiratori Kofun (disambiguation)|lt=Shiratori Kofun (Takarazuka)|ja|白鳥塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun (Takarazuka)|qq=}} == References == {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|鉢塚古墳 (池田市)|version=104109914|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:鉢塚古墳 (池田市)]] {{Commons category|Hachizuka Kofun (Ikeda)}} [[ja:鉢塚古墳 (池田市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11649848}} [[Category:Designated historic sites of Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Hata clan]] [[Category:History of Ikeda, Osaka]] [[Category:Ikeda, Osaka]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]
